title: CSS Filters
description: Apply visual effects like blur, brightness, and shadows with CSS filters.

lessons.title: Blur Filter
lessons.description:
The filter property applies visual effects to elements. The blur() function creates a Gaussian blur effect.

filter: blur(4px);

Higher values create more blur. This is great for backgrounds or creating depth.
lessons.task: Blur the background image to create a frosted-glass effect. Use a blur radius between 2px and 8px.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; margin: 0; height: 200px; position: relative; overflow: hidden; } .bg { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: linear-gradient(45deg, coral, gold, steelblue); } .content { position: relative; z-index: 1; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 100%; } .content h2 { color: white; text-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); margin: 0; }

validations.message: Use the <kbd>filter</kbd> property with the <kbd>blur()</kbd> function. Try a value between 2px and 8px

lessons.title: Grayscale Filter
lessons.description:
The grayscale() function removes color from an element. Use values from 0% (full color) to 100% (fully grayscale).

filter: grayscale(100%);

Great for hover effects or disabled states.
lessons.task: Remove all color from the image to create a black-and-white effect.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .photo { width: 200px; height: 150px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, coral 0%, gold 50%, steelblue 100%); border-radius: 8px; }

validations.message: Which filter function removes color from an element? Set it to full strength

lessons.title: Brightness Filter
lessons.description:
The brightness() function adjusts how bright an element appears. Values below 100% darken, above 100% brighten.

filter: brightness(150%);
lessons.task: Make the card appear brighter and more vivid. Use a brightness value between 110% and 150%.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; background: #1a1a2e; } .card { padding: 2rem; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #4a4a6a, #2a2a4a); border-radius: 12px; text-align: center; } .card span { color: gold; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 2px; }

validations.message: Use the <kbd>filter</kbd> property with the <kbd>brightness()</kbd> function. Values above 100% make things brighter

lessons.title: Drop Shadow
lessons.description:
The drop-shadow() filter creates a shadow that follows the shape of the element, including transparency. Unlike box-shadow, it works on images with transparent backgrounds.

filter: drop-shadow(2px 4px 6px black);
lessons.task: Add a soft shadow behind the star to give it depth. Use the drop-shadow filter with offset, blur, and a color.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 2rem; display: flex; justify-content: center; } .icon { font-size: 4rem; color: gold; }

validations.message: Use the <kbd>filter</kbd> property with <kbd>drop-shadow()</kbd>. It needs horizontal offset, vertical offset, blur radius, and a color
